jayden-woods.bio: Best player on the defense. JACK in Brad White's 3-3-5 — the edge pressure package runs through him.
jayden-woods.stats: Scheme centerpiece at JACK — sets the edge and rushes the passer.
strengths: Explosive first step off the edge
High motor and finish rate as a pass rusher
Versatile enough to drop into short zones
weaknesses: Can be sealed on power runs without length leverage
Still refining counter moves vs. elite tackles
jayden-woods.projection: All-SEC caliber edge defender with NFL draft ceiling if he stays healthy through 2026.
jayden-woods.schemeFit: Ideal JACK in Brad White's 3-3-5 — primary blitzer and edge setter in the odd front.

cormani-mcclain.bio: Lakeland product and former #1 CB recruit nationally. Portal addition who returns as the starting LCB.
cormani-mcclain.stats: Elite lockdown corner and boundary defender.
strengths: Rare length and twitch for the position
Ball skills and recovery speed in phase
weaknesses: Physicality vs. bigger receivers can waver
cormani-mcclain.projection: First-round NFL corner projection if he re-establishes top-tier form in 2026.
cormani-mcclain.schemeFit: Press-man boundary corner — perfect fit for aggressive single-high looks.

jadan-baugh.bio: Workhorse three-down back. Must-win games run through his ability to control time of possession.
strengths: Vision and patience between the tackles
weaknesses: Durability through a heavy workload is the question
jadan-baugh.projection: SEC starter with 1,000-yard season upside in a balanced offense.
jadan-baugh.schemeFit: Every-down back in a pro-style/run-first scheme — early-down and short-yardage hammer.

eric-singleton-jr.bio: Auburn transfer and clear WR1. Go-to weapon on early downs and in the red zone.
strengths: Route nuance and separation at all three levels
weaknesses: Contested catch rate vs. length can vary
eric-singleton-jr.projection: 1,000-yard receiver and primary target for the new staff in 2026.
eric-singleton-jr.schemeFit: Move-the-chains X/slot hybrid — early-down and red-zone focal point.

vernell-brown-iii.bio: Gainesville legacy and starting F/slot receiver. Quickness and vision make him the top return option.
vernell-brown-iii.stats: Slot weapon at F · Versatile route runner.

dallas-wilson.bio: Tampa Bay Tech product and projected X receiver with size to win on the boundary.

tramell-jones-jr.bio: Jacksonville product and dual-threat spring starter candidate with game experience.

dijon-johnson.bio: Press-man corner with physical coverage skills. Rotates with McClain in the secondary.

myles-graham.bio: Gainesville native and All-SEC candidate with speed and instinct in space.

lacota-dippre.bio: James Madison transfer and starting Y tight end. Legitimate vertical weapon in the offense.
lacota-dippre.stats: Starting Y tight end · Red-zone threat.

aaron-philo.bio: Georgia Tech transfer with a cleaner pro-style delivery. Strong on third downs in spring work.
aaron-philo.stats: GT transfer · Rhythm passer in QB battle.

jaden-robinson.bio: Lake City native and MIKE linebacker. Communication and pre-snap reads anchor the defense.

bryce-thornton.bio: Strong safety enforcer and physical leader in the secondary.
bryce-thornton.stats: SS enforcer · Physical leader in secondary.

kanye-clark.bio: UCLA transfer projected as the starting STAR/nickel in the 3-3-5.

cam-dooley.bio: Kentucky transfer who projects as a starting safety in the new scheme.

evan-pryor.bio: Ohio State/Cincinnati transfer RB who adds experienced depth behind Baugh.

tj-shanahan-jr.bio: Penn State/TAMU transfer competing for a starting guard spot on the rebuilt OL.
tj-shanahan-jr.stats: Portal RG · Dead heat for starting job.

eagan-boyer.bio: Penn State transfer with length at tackle. Push for RT or swing tackle role.

harrison-moore.bio: Southlake Carroll transfer who projects as the starting center.

brendan-bett.bio: Baylor transfer nose tackle — key piece in Brad White's 3-3-5 front.
